---
title: "Dynamic Report on statistical modeling to infer differential abundance in MS-based proteomics using the msqrob2gui App"
author: "msqrob2gui"
date: "Developed by [statOmics](https://statomics.github.io)"
output:
    html_document:
      code_download: true
      theme: cosmo
      toc: true
      toc_float: true
      highlight: tango
      number_sections: true
    pdf_document:
      toc: true
      number_sections: true
linkcolor: blue
urlcolor: blue
citecolor: blue

---

<a rel="license" href="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0"><img alt="Creative Commons License" style="border-width:0" src="https://i.creativecommons.org/l/by-nc-sa/4.0/88x31.png" /></a>


# Background
Describe data here. 


Load Packages.

```{r load-pkgs, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(tidyverse)
library(limma)
library(QFeatures)
library(msqrob2)
library(knitr)
})
```

# Input parameters copied from msqrob2gui

Initialize all input parameters used in the GUI. 

## Read Qfeatures

```{r qfFile}
{{qfFile}}
```

Specification of qfeature file.  Note that the qfeature file is copied to the zip file for the report and is named
qfeaturesFile.rds respectively. 




## Model tab

```{r defModel}
{{model}}
doRidge <- ifelse(doRidge=="1", TRUE, FALSE)
doRobust <- ifelse(doRobust=="1", TRUE, FALSE)
```

- The data are modelled using the following formula: form=`r form`

- The parameters are estimated using robust ridge regression if the argument doRidge equals TRUE, otherwise robust regresion without ridge penalization. Here, doRidge=`r doRidge`

## Inference tab

```{r}
{{inference}}
```

- The statistical test assesses the null hypothesis specified using the following contrast for each summarized features: `r contrast`.
- Summarized features are returned as significant using the $\alpha=`r sigLevel`$ FDR-level.


## Report Tab

In the report lists of significant proteins and volcano plots are given. 

# Data import 

We first import the data from FeaturesFile.txt file. This is the file containing
your raw Feature-level intensities. 

To import the data we use the `QFeatures` package.

```{r readQf}
qf <- readRDS(qfeaturesFile)
```

# Data Analysis

## Estimation

We model the summarized feature level expression values using `msqrob`.
By default `msqrob2` estimates the model parameters using robust regression.

We will model the data using following formula

```{r}
as.formula(form)
```

```{r, warning=FALSE}
qf <- msqrob(object = qf, i = selectedSet, formula = as.formula(form), ridge = doRidge, robust = doRobust, overwrite=TRUE)
```

## Inference

We can also explore the design of the model that we specified using the the package `ExploreModelMatrix` 

```{r}
library(ExploreModelMatrix)

if (any(grepl("\\|",attr(terms(as.formula(form)), "term.labels")))){
        visDesign <- VisualizeDesign(colData(qf),update(as.formula(form), as.formula(paste("~. -",paste0("(",attr(terms(as.formula(form)), "term.labels")[grepl("\\|", attr(terms(as.formula(form)), "term.labels"))], ")")))))
} else {
        visDesign <- VisualizeDesign(colData(qf),form)
}

visDesign$plotlist
```

The following contrast(s) will be assessed for each summarized feature:

```{r}
contrast
```

```{r}

if(doRidge==1 ){
    #Intercept is not penalized, this way we get the correct parameter names of the fixed effects
    parameter_names <- paste0("ridge",colnames(visDesign[[3]]))
    parameter_names <- gsub("ridge(Intercept)", "(Intercept)",parameter_names)
} else {
    parameter_names <- colnames(visDesign[[3]])
}

L <- makeContrast(
  contrast,
  parameterNames = parameter_names
  )

qf <- hypothesisTest(object = qf, i = selectedSet, contrast = L, overwrite = TRUE)
```

All `r ncol(L)` contrast(s) above are assessed automatically in this single call to `hypothesisTest`.

## Results 

For every contrast we report a table the significant features at the `r sigLevel` FDR-level.

```{r, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
inferences <- 
  msqrobCollect(qf[[selectedSet]], L) 

for (j in colnames(L)) {

  inference <- inferences |> 
    dplyr::filter(adjPval < sigLevel & contrast == j)

  cat("**Median - Contrast:**", j, "= 0 (", nrow(inference),
      "significant precursors)\n\n")

  cat('<div style="max-height:300px; overflow-y:auto;">')

  print(
    kable(
      inference |>
        dplyr::arrange(pval) |>
        dplyr::relocate(feature),
      row.names = FALSE
    )
  )

  cat('</div>')

  cat("\n\n\n---\n\n")
}
```

Next, we make volcanoplots for each contrast

```{r}
plotVolcano(inferences, significanceLevel = sigLevel) + 
  facet_wrap(~contrast) + 
  labs(title = "DA features")
```


Plot of the number of significant features per contrast: 

```{r}
inferences |> 
  filter(adjPval < sigLevel) |> 
  mutate(DA = sign(logFC) |> as.factor() |> recode("-1"= "down","1" = "up")) |>
  group_by(contrast, DA) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = contrast)) +
  geom_bar(aes(fill = factor(DA)),
           colour = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90))
```


# Interactive visualisation is possible with the altSEE app.

All the code chunks below are set at eval=FALSE. 
So you have to run these manually as they allow interactive visualization.
Note, that the qfeatures object also needs to be imported in your session: 
So run all code chunks below before the interactive visualisation. 

The following packages are needed for the visualization. 
The R chunk below is not evaluated by default. 
If you need/want to install the packages run the code chunk below manually.

```{r install-deps, eval=FALSE}
bioc_pkgs <- c("scater", "iSEE", "iSEEu")
missing_bioc <- bioc_pkgs[!vapply(bioc_pkgs, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(missing_bioc) > 0) {
  if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
  BiocManager::install(missing_bioc)
}

if (!requireNamespace("altSEE", quietly = TRUE)) {
  if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes")
  remotes::install_github("statomics/altSEE")
}
```

We first convert the QFeatures object in a SingleCellExperiment

1. We get the selected set with colData, convert it to a 
   SingleCellExperiment object and add a reduced dimensionality plot. 
2. Unpack inference columns 

```{r qfToSce1, eval=FALSE}
library(scater)
library(QFeatures)
sce <- getWithColData(qf, i = selectedSet) |> 
   as("SingleCellExperiment") |> 
   runMDS(exprs_values = 1) # 1. 
rowData(sce) <- rowData(sce) |> 
  as.data.frame() |> 
  dplyr::select(where(is.atomic)) #2.  
```

Add altExps with peptide data. 

1. We get the peptide sets, convert them to a 
   SingleCellExperiment object and add a reduced dimensionality plot. 
2. Unpack inference columns 

```{r qfToSce2, eval=FALSE}
setNames <- names(qf)
setNames <- setNames[setNames != selectedSet]
for (i in setNames)
{
altExp(sce,i) <- qf[[i]] |> 
     as("SingleCellExperiment") |> 
   runMDS(exprs_values = 1) # 1. 
rowData(altExp(sce,i)) <- rowData(altExp(sce,i)) |> 
  as.data.frame() |> 
  dplyr::select(where(is.atomic)) #2.  
}
```


Setup iSEE panels 

```{r iseePanels, eval=FALSE}
library(iSEE)
library(iSEEu)
library(altSEE)

rdp <- ReducedDimensionPlot(PanelWidth = 6L)
ardp <- AltReducedDimensionPlot(PanelWidth = 6L)
vp <- VolcanoPlot(PanelWidth = 6L)
avp <- AltVolcanoPlot(PanelWidth = 6L)
rdt <- RowDataTable(RowSelectionSource = "VolcanoPlot1", PanelWidth = 6L)
ardt <- AltRowDataTable(RowSelectionSource = "AltVolcanoPlot1", 
                           PanelWidth = 6L)
mlfap <- LinkedFeaturesAssayPlot(
  SelectionExperiment = "(Main)",
  Experiment = "(Main)",
  AltAssay = "proteins",
  YAxisFeatureSource = "RowDataTable1",
  PlotType = "Scatter + lines",
  XAxis = "Column data",
  XAxisColumnData = "sampleId",
  PanelWidth = 6L
  )

alfap <- LinkedFeaturesAssayPlot(
  YAxisFeatureSource = "RowDataTable1",
  SelectionExperiment = "(Main)",
  PlotType = "Scatter + lines",
  XAxis = "Column data",
  XAxisColumnData = "sampleId",
  PanelWidth = 6L
  )

initial_panels <- list(rdp, ardp, vp, avp, rdt, ardt, mlfap, alfap)
```


Launch the app 

```{r launch-app, eval=FALSE}
app <- iSEE(
    sce,
    initial = initial_panels
)

if (interactive()) {
    shiny::runApp(app, launch.browser = TRUE)
}
```

# Session Info

With respect to reproducibility, it is highly recommended to include a session info in your script so that readers of your output can see your particular setup of R. 

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
